primary_reason: The study uses serological testing of febrile patients to quantify chikungunya infections missed by routine regional reporting.
The study evaluates oral prescription opioid use and opioid-use diagnoses as indicators for targeting HCV testing and improving surveillance.
The population-based serosurvey adjusts for sampling structure and test performance to estimate SARS-CoV-2 burden and infection fatality ratios.
The interrupted time-series analysis compares observed STI notifications with counterfactual expected counts to assess pandemic-related underdiagnosis and underreporting.
The scoping review synthesizes behavioral and organizational causes of livestock-disease underreporting and prescribes principles for improving passive surveillance.
The article briefly describes olfactory dysfunction as a COVID-19 marker without developing or evaluating a measurement or surveillance method.
The subcommittee report identifies diagnostic and surveillance deficiencies for ehrlichiosis and anaplasmosis and recommends active clinical, vector, and electronic-record surveillance.
The study combines diagnostic testing, viral sequencing, and phylogenetic inference to characterize dengue circulation hidden by low routine incidence reports.
The linked incarceration and mortality cohort provides a transferable design for estimating underreported communicable-disease mortality during and after detention.
The systematic review uses random-effects meta-analysis to synthesize STI and unprotected-sex prevalence in an underreported population.
The study evaluates a self-controlled tree-temporal scan method for detecting predefined and unexpected clusters of adverse events after vaccination.
The study compares oral, genital, and rectal chlamydia testing to demonstrate infections missed by genital-only screening.
The baseline cross-sectional analysis reports standard regression associations with HIV, HSV-2, pregnancy, and sexual activity without a reusable measurement or modeling contribution.
The facility survey estimates pulmonary tuberculosis underreporting to the national system and identifies patient and facility groups with elevated omission rates.
The paired prospective and retrospective analyses compare RSV with influenza and quantify diagnoses missed or omitted from adult hospital records.
The study tabulates spontaneous adverse-drug-reaction reports and their suspected drugs without evaluating a transferable surveillance or correction method.
The national tuberculosis cohort audits completeness of HIV testing fields and relates missing testing or recording to treatment outcomes.
The study combines representative infection surveys with reported deaths and excess-mortality adjustments to estimate context-specific COVID-19 fatality rates.
The analysis compares laboratory-surveillance isolates with incidence-based expected case counts to estimate pneumococcal surveillance capture across six countries.
The study validates cross-sectional telephone surveys against clinician reports and Google Trends as an alternative source for influenza dynamics and reporting rates.
The paper develops GInPipe, validates genome-only incidence reconstruction against simulated outbreaks and phylodynamic analyses, and demonstrates its surveillance use.
The letter discusses concurrent Zika and COVID-19 threats and offers general control recommendations without substantive method content.
The study guide provides a general history of mpox and mentions suspected underreporting without research, methodological guidance, or surveillance evaluation.
The article discusses programmatic barriers to cholera vaccination without presenting a substantive estimator, evaluation design, or surveillance method.
The study applies a semi-Bayesian bias correction for incomplete testing and imperfect accuracy to estimate state-level COVID-19 prevalence.
The systematic review assesses digital and open-data methods for underreported food-toxin risk analysis, a framework transferable to foodborne infectious-outbreak surveillance.
The age- and sex-stratified serosurvey compares measured hepatitis A immunity with vaccination histories to identify probable unrecorded infection or vaccination.
The randomized comparison quantifies the relative sensitivity and representativeness of facility-only versus facility-plus-household One Health surveillance.
The article narrates community mobilization and advocacy for Latino communities without evaluating a transferable epidemiologic method.
The study develops hospitalization- and death-based underreporting estimators and uses an SEIR model to assess resulting changes in vaccination-strategy projections.
The article discusses norovirus control priorities during COVID-19 without developing or evaluating a surveillance or transmission method.
The nationally representative age-stratified serosurvey uses probability sampling and weights to quantify SARS-CoV-2 infections missed by reported cases.
The study validates self-reported marijuana use against urinalysis, a measurement design transferable to validating self-reported behaviors that affect infectious-disease risk.
The microsimulation represents lifetime Lyme disease infection, diagnosis, sequelae, and quality-adjusted survival to estimate population burden.
The study links laboratory surveillance with administrative encounters to capture possible invasive pneumococcal disease omitted from confirmed-case surveillance.
The retracted narrative concerns intimate partner violence during pandemic restrictions and supplies no concrete infectious-disease measurement transfer route.
The study validates maternal vaccination recall against home-based cards using agreement, sensitivity, specificity, and predictors of recall bias.
The systematic review synthesizes reported pediatric lichen planus after vaccination as a vaccine-safety evidence assessment.
The comparative qualitative study constructs organizational models explaining contrasting livestock-disease reporting rates and information flows.
The dynamic transmission and economic model projects cases, deaths, sequelae, costs, and quality-adjusted survival under infant meningococcal vaccination.
The report extracts operational lessons for building field-epidemiology capacity, surveillance, contact tracing, and outbreak response in conflict settings.
The calibrated natural-history model estimates pediatric tuberculosis incidence and age-, country-, and year-specific case-detection ratios across 185 countries.
The paper develops a hierarchical N-mixture disease-dynamics model with binomial thinning and compares maximum-likelihood and Bayesian fitting approaches.
Cognitive interviews evaluate comprehension and social-desirability limitations of transactional-sex questions intended to measure an important HIV risk exposure.
The study compares laboratory syndromic surveillance with traditional notifications to detect co-circulating arboviruses and surveillance misclassification.
The single dengue pancreatitis case report adds clinical description but no transferable epidemiologic or surveillance method.
The study validates single-item trauma questions against behaviorally specific violence measures among people entering HIV care and quantifies resulting underclassification.
The community serosurvey contrasts measured SARS-CoV-2 antibody prevalence with reported cases and uses the estimate to calculate infection fatality.
The paper evaluates Gaussian-process regularization and time-varying compartmental parameters as strategies for stabilizing epidemic forecasts from uncertain data.
The study proposes a time-dependent susceptible-infected-recovered model to estimate unreported COVID-19 infections and deaths from scarce routine data.
The vaccine-safety case series reports thrombosis-with-thrombocytopenia counts and rates without developing or evaluating a reusable surveillance method.
The study uses population serologic measurements to estimate underreported SARS-CoV-2 infections and compare antibody persistence across targets.
The birth cohort uses longitudinal symptom diaries to capture mild childhood respiratory infections that conventional assessments may miss.
The systematic review and meta-analysis pools clinical estimates of vaccine effectiveness against Delta infection and severe outcomes.
The multicountry audit evaluates acute-flaccid-paralysis surveillance indicators, laboratory performance, enterovirus typing, and vaccine coverage over six years.
This report describes an incidental genotype detection without evaluating a transferable surveillance method.
The paper proposes an extensible framework using healthcare-worker infections as a comparator for evaluating underreporting effects on epidemic space-time patterns.
The analysis integrates underreporting-adjusted seroprevalence and vaccination coverage to estimate state-level presumed SARS-CoV-2 immunity.
The refined mechanistic transmission model estimates detection changes, intervention effects, false positives, and future gambiense trypanosomiasis trajectories.
The regional meeting report compares pertussis diagnosis, surveillance, and immunization practices and issues country-relevant surveillance and vaccination recommendations.
The comparative qualitative case study uses a socioecological framework to identify multilevel determinants of local-government epidemic response.
The pharmacovigilance analysis uses reporting odds ratios to compare bacterial and herpesvirus infection signals across biologic therapies.
The proposed classification concerns a rare chromosomal syndrome and provides no concrete route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
The study links HIV records with death certificates to quantify omitted HIV attribution and identify predictors of mortality underreporting.
The analysis combines prospective mortality-risk prediction with historical and concurrent matched comparisons to estimate context-specific COVID-19 excess deaths.
The article speculates about pandemic-related Lassa fever underreporting and resource allocation without a substantive analytic method.
The paper develops and externally validates a scalable open-data epidemic risk index incorporating distance-sampling correction for underreported infections.
The study compares temporal fragments of global cholera spatial distributions as a mapping approach for infectious-disease surveillance.
The ecological study reports standard regression correlations between district characteristics and COVID-19 incidence or mortality without a distinct reusable method.
The systematic review synthesizes infectious, toxicological, occupational, and traumatic hazards affecting children in artisanal gold mining.
The SARS-CoV-2 transmission model is fitted jointly to repeated serosurveys and death series to infer attack rates and death-reporting completeness.
The multicountry review identifies policy-practice gaps and prescribes priorities for detecting, reporting, preventing, and treating pediatric tuberculosis.
The paper extends SEIR modeling to incorporate diagnostic false negatives and symptom-based test selection, validates effects through simulations, and supplies an R package.
The anonymous survey jointly measures occupational bloodborne-pathogen exposures, reporting completeness, and reasons for nonreporting among dental trainees.
The evaluation applies CDC surveillance-system guidelines to assess usefulness, completeness, timeliness, acceptability, and underreporting in vaccine-safety surveillance.
The cross-sectional questionnaire reports needlestick incidence, knowledge, and practices without evaluating a distinct measurement or correction method.
The surveillance-record audit measures field completeness and adverse-event reporting rates across locations, vaccines, and reaction types.
The study uses a conventional cross-sectional questionnaire to describe rabies knowledge and practices without a transferable surveillance or measurement innovation.
The small neurological adverse-event case series provides clinical descriptions but no methodological evaluation or surveillance correction.
The cross-country analysis constructs a stochastic-frontier pandemic-containment score and relates it to subsequent economic growth.
The prospective study compares air, surface, and wastewater SARS-CoV-2 signals with later clinical diagnoses as an early-warning surveillance design.
The study validates pharmacy dispensing as an auxiliary tuberculosis surveillance source and uses seasonal ARIMA forecasts to assess an unexpected notification decline.
The paper develops a Bayesian hierarchical spatial count model for false-negative and false-positive reporting and provides expert-prior elicitation functions.
The analysis uses standard regression to describe social vulnerability, comorbidities, tuberculosis, and COVID-19 outcomes without a reusable method contribution.
The anticoagulant-related nephropathy case report addresses a noninfectious clinical condition without a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The Bayesian spatial analysis combines prevalence-survey and notification data to map district-level tuberculosis prevalence-to-notification gaps.
The population-based case-control study uses propensity-score adjustment to assess birth-defect safety after influenza vaccination during etiologic exposure windows.
The systematic review synthesizes the geographic, clinical, diagnostic, reservoir, and treatment evidence for human dirofilariasis in Sri Lanka.
The ethics commentary argues that Latino COVID-19 burden is undercounted but presents no substantive epidemiologic method.
The study develops an ensemble framework that corrects multiple biased surveillance inputs and combines modeled detection, hospitalization, and fatality ratios to reconstruct infections.
The systematic search synthesizes African invasive-candidiasis cases, diagnostic approaches, species distributions, treatment access, and resistance patterns.
The multispecies serosurvey measures exposure to four zoonotic pathogens in dromedaries to inform One Health monitoring.
The unselected autopsy cohort screens for SARS-CoV-2 and respiratory coinfections to quantify infections and deaths missed before death.
The study validates national registry reporting against prospectively confirmed periprosthetic joint infections and models factors associated with capture.
The article describes STI notification trends and geographic disparities without directly evaluating a reusable adjustment or surveillance method.
The Global Burden of Disease analysis uses joinpoint trends and Gaussian-process regression to estimate PM2.5-attributable neonatal infection burden across countries.
The representative serosurvey explicitly incorporates durable antibody-test performance and infection-to-seroconversion and infection-to-death delays into burden estimates.
The multicenter comparison quantifies antimicrobial-resistance underreporting caused by delayed adoption of updated carbapenem susceptibility breakpoints.
The paper develops an excess-mortality framework for reconstructing SARS-CoV-2 transmission, attack rates, fatality, and reporting completeness.
The systematic and clinical review constructs validated sequelae maps and selection rules for incorporating meningococcal outcomes into economic evaluations.
The study combines national and global pharmacovigilance reports with a literature review to assess adverse events during HIV pre-exposure prophylaxis.
The Google-search forecasting study concerns intimate partner violence, and pandemic timing alone does not establish a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The natural experiment compares reopened and closed districts to isolate the effect of mandatory school testing on COVID-19 case ascertainment.
The study reports local mandatory-notification incidence trends without evaluating notification sensitivity or introducing a transferable analytic design.
The paper develops and evaluates a topic-modeling and classification pipeline for extracting vaccine adverse-event mentions from social media.
The Bayesian diagnostic-accuracy meta-analysis compares rapid cerebrospinal-fluid PCR assays and identifies when negative results require additional testing.
The ecological analysis estimates SARS-CoV-2 underdiagnosis from excess severe-respiratory cases with undefined etiologies relative to historical baselines.
The review uses seroprevalence and immune-response evidence to recommend vaccination priorities for highly exposed African populations with constrained vaccine supply.
The study primarily describes multiple-sclerosis activity during the pandemic and offers no distinct infectious-disease method beyond standard association analysis.
The study combines individual case data, subdistrict space-time analysis, and time-varying reproduction numbers to evaluate targeted and citywide interventions.
The study validates wastewater estimates against randomized community sampling and compares their prevalence accuracy with clinically reported cases.
The multicountry facility network establishes standardized baseline rates for pregnancy and neonatal safety outcomes and assesses outcome underreporting.
The study uses dense regional sequencing, transmission-lineage reconstruction, and reproduction-number estimation to characterize HIV epidemic growth and underreporting.
The systematic review and random-effects meta-analysis estimates needlestick exposure and reporting practices among dental students across countries.
The standard regression analysis concerns prescription-opioid abuse and dependence and supplies no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The study applies a standardized WHO case-control protocol to identify modifiable infection-control determinants of occupational SARS-CoV-2 infection.
The surveillance analysis reports neurologic, ocular, and otic manifestations among syphilis cases without developing a correction or measurement method.
The study contrasts prevaccine and vaccine-era incidence, adjusts for underreporting, and estimates disease cases averted by routine childhood immunization.
The review centers on sexual violence screening and sequelae, and its mention of STI risk does not supply a concrete infectious-epidemiology method.
The hospital study describes vulvar dermatosis prevalence and standard risk-factor associations without a reusable infectious-disease design contribution.
The longitudinal network study measures how name prompting recovers omitted ties and changes network metrics used in transmission and diffusion analyses.
The workshop report prescribes a harmonized, linked, geographically diverse framework for active surveillance of antiretroviral safety during pregnancy.
The study classifies severe respiratory admissions by known and unknown etiology and models profiles associated with possible COVID-19 underreporting.
The analysis describes vaccination, incidence, and mortality trends in Indigenous populations without a distinct causal or surveillance-adjustment method.
The study develops and validates machine-learning and phenomenological models that convert wastewater signals into community SARS-CoV-2 prevalence estimates.
The multicenter qualitative audit uses systematic thematic analysis of hospital deaths to identify patient- and health-system determinants of COVID-19 mortality.
The review links diagnostic sensitivity and asymptomatic infection to chlamydia underreporting and recommends a national nucleic-acid-based screening program.
The convenience-sample survey reports tuberculosis knowledge, attitudes, and practices using standard regression without a reusable measurement contribution.
The study assembles cross-pathogen empirical reporting rates and develops a model for predicting and correcting underreporting from pathogen and country characteristics.
The article broadly discusses COVID-19 effects on African leishmaniasis control without substantive analytic or methodological content.
The review compares Clostridioides difficile detection, susceptibility, and One Health evidence and identifies surveillance and antimicrobial-use priorities for Southeast Asia.
The study demonstrates large demographic bias from using aggregate test positivity to correct RSV underascertainment and recommends subgroup-specific adjustments.
The paper develops a stochastic network SIR model that jointly estimates transmission and underreporting and supports vaccination and distancing counterfactuals.
The study applies prospective space-time scan statistics to detect emerging municipal COVID-19 clusters and estimate their relative risks.
The stochastic state-space SIRD model permits time-varying infection, mortality, and reporting rates, reinfection, and uncertainty-calibrated forecasting.
The study merges notification, laboratory, and encounter records to quantify chlamydia and gonorrhea capture gaps and improve burden estimates.
The longitudinal pediatric study compares several serological platforms and repeated antibody measurements to detect unreported SARS-CoV-2 infections.
The national data-quality assessment triangulates multiple routine sources to distinguish tuberculosis undernotification from failures to report notified cases.
The compartmental model estimates undocumented infections and effective reproduction numbers from reported cases, deaths, and epidemiological parameters.
The study analyzes adolescent suicidality with standard survey associations and provides no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The study enhances and validates a claims-based algorithm by linkage with the HIV registry to identify transgender people living with HIV.
The post-Ebola report documents capacity-building strategies and operational lessons for improving surveillance completeness, reliability, and sustainability in resource-limited settings.
The study validates colistin broth disk elution against reference broth microdilution for Acinetobacter susceptibility testing in low-resource laboratories.
The sequencing study demonstrates that majority-consensus reporting omits transmissible within-host SARS-CoV-2 diversity and recommends more complete variant reporting.
The study calibrates and simulates vaccine- and age-structured transmission models to evaluate COVID-19 burden and prioritization strategies.
The study reconstructs epidemic differential equations and contact numbers to assess African COVID-19 underreporting, nonlinear dynamics, and mitigation effects.
The cross-sectional HIV survey reports prevalence, treatment coverage, and standard risk-factor associations without a distinct reusable method.
The study compares recursive state estimators within a dynamic infectious-process model.
The retracted fever-clinic report cannot serve as a reliable methods record despite describing a local management model.
The study derives multiplicative underascertainment factors from serological and postmortem surveillance to bound national COVID-19 infection burden in Africa.
